# Provider Adapter Implementations

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providerruntimeboundarydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to runtime actor boundary](../runtime-actor-boundary.md)

Provider capability, protocol, and priority are owned by public `riido-contracts/docs/20-domain/provider-capability.md`. This surface records only how provider adapters appear inside the C4 Provider Runtime context.

- RIID-4658: `ClaudeStreamJSONAdapter` lives under `internal/provider/claude`.
- RIID-4659: `CodexAppServerAdapter` lives under `internal/provider/codex`.
- RIID-4660: `OpenClawAgentJSONAdapter` lives under `internal/provider/openclaw`.
- RIID-4661: `CursorAgentStreamJSONAdapter` lives under `internal/provider/cursor`.

| Adapter | Runtime representation | Primary draft category |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ClaudeStreamJSONAdapter` | `claude -p --output-format stream-json` stdout is consumed as NDJSON; session id is extracted from the `system.init` line. | Mostly Cat C |
| `CodexAppServerAdapter` | `codex --sandbox danger-full-access app-server --listen stdio://` JSON-RPC; daemon owns the full-access harness envelope. | Cat C plus approval |
| `OpenClawAgentJSONAdapter` | `openclaw agent --local --json`; unsupported CLI folds into unavailable through calendar-version gate. | Mostly Cat C |
| `CursorAgentStreamJSONAdapter` | `cursor-agent -p --output-format stream-json` root-print shape; version/profile differences require explicit launch profile. | Mostly Cat C |

All four adapters implement the same `Provider` port and emit the same `ProviderEventDraft` surface. Adapter-specific branching is by `ProtocolKind` only.
